name: Donald Byrd
description: American recording artist; jazz and rhythm & blues trumpeter and vocalist (1932–2013)
abstract: Donaldson Toussaint L'Ouverture Byrd II (December 9, 1932 – February 4, 2013) was an American jazz and rhythm & blues trumpeter, composer and vocalist. A sideman for many other jazz musicians of his generation, Byrd was one of the few hard bop musicians who successfully explored funk and soul while remaining a jazz artist.

about_quotes.speaker: Herbie Hancock
about_quotes.evidence: Donald really took me under his wing... he was like really a big brother to me. And he really showed me the ropes in New York and he really encouraged me in so many ways and taught me a lot of things.
about_quotes.doc_title: Herbie Hancock interviewed by Michael Woods, New York City, New York, November 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Carl Allen
about_quotes.evidence: I remember at some point in the nineties I did a record with Donald Byrd called “A City Called Heaven,” and Bobby Hutcherson, Joe Henderson, Rufus Reid was on it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carl Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, Georgia, January 4, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: Donald Byrd played with on Wailing With Lou... Lou Takes Off, which is a sextet with Curtis Fuller, Donald Byrd and Sonny Clark
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: My chief mentor at the time was Donald Byrd. I studied with him when I was fourteen years old.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: But, Donald was a great mentor to me; he was like a second father.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: Later on, we hired Donald Byrd, made it a quintet. ... I enjoyed that, working with Paul and watching Paul develop... But at first it was a quartet, for many months.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
